Core Details:**

The March 2026 Consumer Price Index data, released on April 10, 2026, confirmed **headline inflation surged to 3.3% year-over-year**, marking the largest annual increase in nearly two years[5]. signals.headline: Market Impact:**
signals.raw:
Market Impact:**

This data significantly alters inflation expectations and rate-cut probabilities. Goldman Sachs raised its December 2026 headline PCE inflation forecast by a full percentage point since the war began[1]. signals.headline: Core inflation** remained elevated at 2
signals.raw: Core inflation** remained elevated at 2.6% year-over-year, above pre-pandemic comfort levels[5]. This dual pressure—both headline and core inflation staying high—makes the Federal Reserve cautious about rate cuts[5].
